lede: A look at October 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Bruno Mars and Koji Murofushi, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States Department of Homeland Security
events.desc: U.S. president George W. Bush announces the establishment of the Office of Homeland Security.

events.title: Solidarity (Polish trade union)
events.desc: Poland bans Solidarity and all other trade unions.

events.title: Che Guevara
events.desc: Guerrilla leader Che Guevara and his men are captured in Bolivia. Guevara would be executed the next day.

events.title: Harrow and Wealdstone rail crash
events.desc: The Harrow and Wealdstone rail crash in England kills 112 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Germany annexes western Poland.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Corporal Alvin C. York kills 28 German soldiers and captures 132, for which he was awarded the Medal of Honor.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Confederate invasion of Kentucky is halted at the Battle of Perryville.

events.title: Great Stand on the Ugra River
events.desc: The Great Stand on the Ugra River puts an end to Tatar rule over Moscow

events.title: Council of Chalcedon
events.desc: The first session of the Council of Chalcedon begins.

events.title: Constantine the Great
events.desc: Constantine I defeats Licinius, who loses his European territories.

births.name: The Miz
births.line: American wrestler, actor, and television personality

births.name: Mary Engle Pennington
births.line: American bacteriological chemist and refrigeration engineer

deaths.text: Willy Brandt, German lawyer and politician, 4th Chancellor of Germany, Nobel Prize laureate
Larry Semon, American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Franklin Pierce, American general, lawyer, and politician, 14th President of the United States

observances.text: Christian feast day: Pelagia (Eastern Orthodox and Roman Catholic Churches)
Christian feast day: San Ernesto, Che Guevara as a folk saint. (Bolivian campesinos)
Christian feast day: William Dwight Porter Bliss and Richard T. Ely (Episcopal Church)
